items.front: When must the operational status of ASDE systems be determined?
items.back: During the relief briefing, or as soon as possible after assuming responsibility for the associated position.

items.front: What is ASDE primarily used to augment?
items.back: Visual observation of aircraft landing or departing, and aircraft or vehicular movements on runways, taxiways, and other movement areas.

items.front: What three methods are used to identify an ASDE target?
items.back: 1) Pilot/vehicle operator position report. 2) Controller's visual observation. 3) An identified target on ASR or CTRD.

items.front: What ATC actions may be based on ASDE system derived information?
items.back: Formulating clearances and control instructions, positioning aircraft/vehicles, and determining runway occupancy and movement area status.

items.front: What is the controller's immediate action when an ASDE safety logic alert activates?
items.back: Immediately assess the situation visually and via the ASDE display, then take appropriate action.
